mnemonics: P - Pointer is a variable that holds the memory address of another variable
P - Pointers are used to indirectly access or manipulate variables
keyPoints: Introduction to Pointers: Pointers are a fundamental concept in C programming
Pointer Concepts: Pointers are used to indirectly access or manipulate variables
Accessing Variables through Pointers: Use the asterisk (*) to access variables through pointers
Pointer Applications: Pointers are used in everyday applications such as arrays and linked lists
Dynamic Memory Allocation Functions: Use malloc(), calloc(), realloc(), and free() to manage memory
